title: pelargonium tenellum
intro: Pelargonium tenellum, also known as the Cape Geranium, is a species of flowering plant native to the Western Cape region of South Africa. It is a perennial herb with medicinal properties, traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, coughs, and digestive problems. The plant's health benefits are attributed to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other respiratory infections. Pelargonium tenellum is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture for its medicinal uses, and it may also be added to herbal remedies, such as cough syrups and throat lozenges.
